Question: Complete the proof that $$\underset{x \rightarrow 2}{\text{lim}} \left(\right. 3 x - 2 \left.\right) = 4$$ by filling in the blanks. Let _______. Choose $$\delta = \text{ululululululul} .$$ Assume $$0 < | x - \text{ulululul} | < \text{ulululul} .$$ Thus, $$| \text{ululululululul} - \text{ulululul} | = \text{ulululululululululululululululululululululululululululululul} \varepsilon .$$ Therefore, $$\underset{x \rightarrow 2}{\text{lim}} \left(\right. 3 x - 2 \left.\right) = 4 .$$
Answer: Let $$\varepsilon > 0 ;$$ choose $$\delta = \frac{\varepsilon}{3} ;$$ assume $$0 < | x - 2 | < \delta .$$ Thus, $$| ( 3 x - 2 ) - 4 | = | 3 x - 6 | = | 3 | \cdotp | x - 2 | < 3 \cdotp \delta = 3 \cdotp ( \varepsilon \text{/} 3 ) = \varepsilon .$$ Therefore, $$\underset{x \rightarrow 2}{\text{lim}} 3 x - 2 = 4 .$$

Question: Find δ corresponding to $$\varepsilon > 0$$ for a proof that $$\underset{x \rightarrow 9}{\text{lim}} \sqrt{x} = 3 .$$

Complete the proof that $$\underset{x \rightarrow 1}{\text{lim}} x^{2} = 1 .$$ Let $$\varepsilon > 0 ;$$ choose $$\delta = \text{min} \{ 1 , \varepsilon \text{/} 3 \} ;$$ assume $$0 < | x - 1 | < \delta .$$ Since $$| x - 1 | < 1 ,$$ we may conclude that $$-1 < x - 1 < 1 .$$ Thus, $$1 < x + 1 < 3 .$$ Hence, $$| x + 1 | < 3 .$$
Find $$\delta$$ corresponding to ε for a proof that $$\underset{x \rightarrow 1^{-}}{\text{lim}} \sqrt{1 - x} = 0 .$$
